14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
15	
16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11
24	
25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one

4	def is_legacy_structural_tag(obj: Dict) -> bool:
5	    # test whether an object is a legacy structural tag
6	    # see `StructuralTagResponseFormat` at `sglang.srt.entrypoints.openai.protocol`
7	    if obj.get("structures", None) is not None:
8	        assert obj.get("triggers", None) is not None
9	        return True
10	    else:
11	        assert obj.get("format", None) is not None
12	        return False

14	"""Constrained decoding with llguidance backend."""
15	
16	import json
17	import logging
18	import os
19	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
20	
21	import torch
22	from llguidance import LLMatcher, LLTokenizer, StructTag, grammar_from
23	from llguidance.hf import from_tokenizer
24	from llguidance.torch import (
25	    allocate_token_bitmask,
26	    apply_token_bitmask_inplace,
27	    fill_next_token_bitmask,
28	)
29	
30	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
31	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
32	    BaseGrammarBackend,
33	    BaseGrammarObject,
34	)
35	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag
36	
37	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
38	
39	
40	class GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
41	
42	    def __init__(self, llguidance_tokenizer: LLTokenizer, serialized_grammar: str):
43	        super().__init__()
44	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = llguidance_tokenizer
45	        self.serialized_grammar = serialized_grammar
46	
47	        self.ll_matcher = LLMatcher(
48	            self.llguidance_tokenizer,
49	            self.serialized_grammar,
50	            log_level=int(os.environ.get("LLGUIDANCE_LOG_LEVEL", "1")),
51	        )
52	        self.bitmask = None
53	
54	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
55	        if not self.ll_matcher.consume_token(token):
56	            logger.warning(f"matcher error: {self.ll_matcher.get_error()}")
57	            self.finished = True
58	
59	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
60	        if self.ll_matcher.is_stopped():
61	            self.finished = True
62	
63	        fill_next_token_bitmask(self.ll_matcher, vocab_mask, idx)
64	
65	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
66	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
67	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
68	        if self.bitmask is None or self.bitmask.shape[0] < batch_size:
69	            # only create bitmask when batch gets larger
70	            self.bitmask = allocate_token_bitmask(
71	                batch_size, self.llguidance_tokenizer.vocab_size
72	            )
73	            bitmask = self.bitmask
74	        else:
75	            bitmask = self.bitmask[:batch_size]
76	
77	        return bitmask
78	
79	    @staticmethod
80	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
81	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
85	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace(logits, vocab_mask)
86	
87	    def copy(self):
88	        return GuidanceGrammar(
89	            llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
90	            serialized_grammar=self.serialized_grammar,
91	        )
92	
93	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
94	        ff_tokens = self.ll_matcher.compute_ff_tokens()
95	        if ff_tokens:
96	            return ff_tokens, ""
97	        else:
98	            return None
99	
100	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
101	        return "", -1
102	
103	    def jump_and_retokenize(
104	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
105	    ):
106	        pass

108	
109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:

159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ

161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [
166	                StructTag(
167	                    begin=structure["begin"],
168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],
170	                    trigger=structural_tag["triggers"][0],  # TODO?
171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ

775	    def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
776	        """
777	        Construct sampling param based on default + override values
778	
779	        The default values of sampling are populated in `default_sampling_para` via sgl.function.run(...sampling_args)
780	        , and `sampling_params` contains the override values from sgl.gen().
781	
782	        Here we use default_sampling_para as the base and override the values if they exist in `sampling_params`.
783	        It also extends the stop tokens based on the chat template.
784	        """
785	
786	        # deepcopy is required because the dict has lists inside
787	        clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)
788	
789	        for item in [
790	            "max_new_tokens",
791	            "min_new_tokens",
792	            "n",
793	            "stop",
794	            "stop_token_ids",
795	            "stop_regex",
796	            "temperature",
797	            "top_p",
798	            "top_k",
799	            "min_p",
800	            "frequency_penalty",
801	            "presence_penalty",
802	            "ignore_eos",
803	            "return_logprob",
804	            "logprob_start_len",
805	            "top_logprobs_num",
806	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
807	            "dtype",
808	            "regex",
809	            "json_schema",
810	        ]:
811	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
812	            if value is not None:
813	                setattr(clone, item, value)
814	
815	        if self.chat_template.stop_str:
816	            if clone.stop == ():
817	                clone.stop = []
818	            elif isinstance(clone.stop, str):
819	                clone.stop = [clone.stop]
820	            clone.stop += self.chat_template.stop_str
821	
822	        return clone

835	        if expr is not None:

34	    s += (

36	        + " is a character in Harry Potter. Please fill in the following information about this character.\n"
37	    )
38	    s += "The constrained regex is:\n"

40	    s += "The JSON output is:\n"
41	    s += sgl.gen("json_output", max_tokens=256, regex=character_regex)

46	    print(state.text())

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,

16	import json
17	import logging
18	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
19	
20	import interegular
21	import torch
22	from outlines.fsm.guide import RegexGuide
23	from outlines.models.transformers import TransformerTokenizer
24	from pydantic import BaseModel
25	
26	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
27	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
28	    BaseGrammarBackend,
29	    BaseGrammarObject,
30	)
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_jump_forward import OutlinesJumpForwardMap
32	
33	try:
34	    from outlines.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
35	except ImportError:
36	    from outlines_core.fsm.json_schema import build_regex_from_schema
37	
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state
112	
113	
114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)
191

19	        s += "The following are questions with answers.\n\n"
20	        s += "Q: What is the capital of France?\n"
21	        s += "A: Paris\n"
22	        s += "Q: What is the capital of Germany?\n"
23	        s += "A: Berlin\n"
24	        s += "Q: What is the capital of Italy?\n"
25	        s += "A: Rome\n"
26	        s += "Q: " + question + "\n"
27	        s += "A:" + sgl.gen("answer", stop="\n", temperature=0)
28	
29	    ret = few_shot_qa.run(question="What is the capital of the United States?")
30	    assert "washington" in ret["answer"].strip().lower(), f"answer: {ret['answer']}"
31	
32	    rets = few_shot_qa.run_batch(
33	        [
34	            {"question": "What is the capital of Japan?"},
35	            {"question": "What is the capital of the United Kingdom?"},
36	            {"question": "What is the capital city of China?"},
37	        ],
38	        temperature=0.1,
39	    )
40	    answers = [x["answer"].strip().lower() for x in rets]
41	    assert answers == ["tokyo", "london", "beijing"], f"answers: {answers}"

53	def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
54	    """Convert a JSON schema to a string.
55	    Parameters
56	    ----------
57	    json_schema
58	        The JSON schema.
59	    Returns
60	    -------
61	    str
62	        The JSON schema converted to a string.
63	    Raises
64	    ------
65	    ValueError
66	        If the schema is not a dictionary, a string or a Pydantic class.
67	    """
68	    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
69	        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema)
70	    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
71	        schema_str = json_schema
72	    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
73	        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
74	    else:
75	        raise ValueError(
76	            f"Cannot parse schema {json_schema}. The schema must be either "
77	            + "a Pydantic class, a dictionary or a string that contains the JSON "
78	            + "schema specification"
79	        )
80	    return schema_str

107	def dump_state_text(filename: str, states: list, mode: str = "w"):
108	    """Dump program state in a text file."""
109	    from sglang.lang.interpreter import ProgramState
110	
111	    with open(filename, mode) as fout:
112	        for i, s in enumerate(states):
113	            if isinstance(s, str):
114	                pass
115	            elif isinstance(s, ProgramState):
116	                s = s.text()
117	            else:
118	                s = str(s)
119	
120	            fout.write(
121	                "=" * 40 + f" {i} " + "=" * 40 + "\n" + s + "\n" + "=" * 80 + "\n\n"
122	            )

55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)

145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()

200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:

361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs
196

## SGLang Constraint Implementation Exploration - Complete Report

I've thoroughly explored the SGLang codebase to understand how constraints are implemented end-to-end. Here's what I found:

### 1. **Existing Constraint Types & Where They Are Defined**

All constraint types are defined in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` (lines 55-87):

```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(
        self,
        ...
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,    # JSON Schema constraint
        regex: Optional[str] = None,           # Regex constraint
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,            # EBNF grammar constraint
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,  # Structured tag constraint
        ...
    ):
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.regex = regex
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
```

**Validation** (lines 155-161):
```python
grammars = [
    self.json_schema,
    self.regex,
    self.ebnf,
]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

### 2. **Constraint Validation & Enforcement Architecture**

The constraint enforcement uses a **grammar backend abstraction** pattern with pluggable implementations:

**Base Classes** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`):

- `BaseGrammarBackend` - Abstract backend with dispatch methods
- `BaseGrammarObject` - Grammar state machine interface

**Backend Implementations:**
- **Outlines Backend** (`outlines_backend.py`): Uses `outlines` library's `RegexGuide` for FSM-based token filtering
- **XGrammar Backend** (`xgrammar_backend.py`): Uses `xgrammar` library's `GrammarCompiler` and `GrammarMatcher`
- **LLGuidance Backend** (`llguidance_backend.py`): Uses `llguidance` library's `LLMatcher`

**Grammar Flow** (`grammar_manager.py`, lines 67-105):

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None):
        
        key = (type_str, constraint_value)  # e.g., ("json", schema_str)
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(key)
        req.grammar = value
```

### 3. **Test Files for Constraints**

Test patterns are in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/test/kits/`:

- **`json_constrained_kit.py`** - JSON Schema tests with mixin pattern
- **`regex_constrained_kit.py`** - Regex constraint tests (email, phone, date, hex color, etc.)
- **`ebnf_constrained_kit.py`** - EBNF grammar tests

Example test pattern from `regex_constrained_kit.py` (lines 52-60):
```python
def test_regex_generate_email(self):
    pattern = r"^user@example\.com$"
    response = requests.post(
        self.base_url + "/generate",
        json={
            "text": prompt,
            "sampling_params": {
                "regex": pattern,
                ...
            },
        },
    )
```

### 4. **JSON-Related Utilities**

**Core JSON utility** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/utils.py`, lines 53-80):

```python
def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema)
    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
        schema_str = json_schema
    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
    return schema_str
```

### 5. **JSON Schema Constraint Implementation Details**

**Outlines Backend** (`outlines_backend.py`, lines 145-178):

Uses `outlines` library's regex compilation:
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
    try:
        regex = build_regex_from_object(
            key_string,
            whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
        )
    except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._compile_regex(regex)
```

**XGrammar Backend** (`xgrammar_backend.py`, lines 257-270):

Uses `xgrammar`'s native JSON schema compiler:
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    try:
        if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
        else:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
                schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
            )
    except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
```

### 6. **JSONPath Library Search Results**

**No JSONPath libraries found** - The codebase does not currently import:
- `jsonpath-ng`
- `jsonpath2`
- `jsonpath_rw`
- `jmespath`
- Any other JSONPath libraries

This is expected since JSONPath extraction is different from constraint generation.

---

## Key Pattern for Adding `json_path` Constraint

Based on the codebase analysis, here's the implementation pattern you should follow:

### Step 1: Update `SamplingParams` 
Add to `sampling_params.py`:
```python
json_path: Optional[str] = None  # JSONPath expression
```

### Step 2: Add validation in `verify()`
```python
grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf, self.json_path]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, ebnf, or json_path can be set.")
```

### Step 3: Add dispatch methods in each backend
In `outlines_backend.py`, `xgrammar_backend.py`, and `llguidance_backend.py`:
```python
def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
    # Implementation using JSONPath library of choice
```

### Step 4: Update `grammar_manager.py`
Add handling in `process_req_with_grammar()`:
```python
elif req.sampling_params.json_path is not None:
    key = ("json_path", req.sampling_params.json_path)
```

### Step 5: Add dispatch routing in `base_grammar_backend.py`
Add to `_init_value_dispatch()`:
```python
elif key_type == "json_path":
    grammar = self.dispatch_json_path(key_string)
```

### Step 6: Create test kit
Following the pattern in `json_constrained_kit.py` or `regex_constrained_kit.py`.

```python
def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema)
    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
        schema_str = json_schema
    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
    return schema_str
```

```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
    try:
        regex = build_regex_from_object(
            key_string,
            whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
        )
    except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._compile_regex(regex)
```

**XGrammar Backend** (`xgrammar_backend.py`, lines 257-270):

Uses `xgrammar`'s native JSON schema compiler:
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    try:
        if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
        else:
            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
                schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
            )
    except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:
        logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
        return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
```

198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary report.

## SGLang Output Constraints - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's how SGLang programs define and use output constraints:

---

### 1. **The `gen()` Function & Parameters**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)

The `gen()` function is the main public API that accepts constraint parameters:

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,           # <- REGEX CONSTRAINT
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,     # <- JSON SCHEMA CONSTRAINT
):
```

Key constraint parameters:
- `regex`: Direct regex pattern constraint
- `json_schema`: JSON Schema constraint  
- `dtype`: Type inference (converts to regex: `int`, `float`, `str`, `bool`)
- `choices`: Selection from discrete options (via `SglSelect`)

---

### 2. **The `SglGen` Expression Class**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (lines 451-503)

The `gen()` function returns an `SglGen` object that stores constraints:

```python
class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        max_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        min_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        top_k: Optional[int] = None,
        min_p: Optional[float] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.name = name
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            # ... all params ...
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )
```

---

### 3. **The `SglSamplingParams` Structure (Frontend)**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (lines 17-139)

Frontend-level sampling parameters dataclass:

```python
@dataclasses.dataclass
class SglSamplingParams:
    max_new_tokens: int = 128
    min_new_tokens: int = 0
    n: int = 1
    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = -1
    min_p: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None      # <- CONSTRAINT
    
    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
    dtype: Optional[str] = None             # <- CONSTRAINT
    regex: Optional[str] = None             # <- CONSTRAINT
```

Key method: `to_srt_kwargs()` which serializes to backend-compatible format.

---

### 4. **The `SamplingParams` Structure (Backend)**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` (lines 31-96)

The backend-level SamplingParams with extended constraint support:

```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(
        self,
        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        top_k: int = -1,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
        n: int = 1,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,        # <- CONSTRAINT
        regex: Optional[str] = None,              # <- CONSTRAINT
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,               # <- CONSTRAINT
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,     # <- CONSTRAINT
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> None:
```

Constraint validation (lines 155-161):
```python
grammars = [
    self.json_schema,
    self.regex,
    self.ebnf,
```

```python
def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
```

The server-side `GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()` dispatches constraints to grammar backends:

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        if self.grammar_backend is None:
            error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported..."
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
        else:
            if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
            elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
            elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
            elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
            
            value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                key, req.require_reasoning
            )
            req.grammar = value
```

```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
    
    def _init_value_dispatch(
        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        s = time.perf_counter()
        key_type, key_string = key
        if key_type == "json":
            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
        elif key_type == "regex":
            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
        elif key_type == "ebnf":
            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
        # ... etc
```

- **XGrammar Backend:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` - Uses compiled grammar matchers with token bitmask optimization
- **LLGuidance Backend:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py` - Integrates with llm-guidance library

Constraint-related exports to users:

```python

    function,
    gen,           # <- Main constraint API

    gen_string,
```

There are no direct constraint type classes (like `SglRegex`, `SglJson`) exported—constraints are passed as strings/parameters directly.

### 11. **Real-World Example**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/examples/frontend_language/usage/json_decode.py`

```python
@sgl.function
def character_gen(s, name):
    s += (
        name
        + " is a character in Harry Potter. Please fill in the following information about this character.\n"
    )
    s += sgl.gen("json_output", max_tokens=256, regex=character_regex)

# Usage:
state = character_gen.run(name="Hermione Granger")
```

Constraints can also be built from Pydantic models:

```python
from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import build_regex_from_object

class Wizard(BaseModel):
    name: str
    age: int
    weapon: Weapon

s += sgl.gen(
    "character",
    max_tokens=128,
    temperature=0,
    regex=build_regex_from_object(Wizard),
)
```

---

### Summary

**Constraint Flow:**
1. User calls `sgl.gen(..., regex="...", json_schema="...", dtype=type)`
2. Creates `SglGen` expression with `SglSamplingParams`
3. Interpreter resolves params via `_resolve_sampling_params()`
4. Backend (e.g., `RuntimeEndpoint`) serializes via `to_srt_kwargs()`
5. HTTP request sends `{"sampling_params": {"regex": "...", "json_schema": "..."}}`
6. Server-side `GrammarManager` receives constraints in `SamplingParams`
7. Dispatches to grammar backend (`outlines`, `xgrammar`, `llguidance`)
8. Grammar backend compiles constraint to finite state machine (FSM)
9. During decoding, FSM restricts token generation vocabulary at each step

```python
):
```

Key constraint parameters:

```python
    "gen",         # Users call sgl.gen(..., regex=..., json_schema=...)

    # ... other exports ...
]
```

There are no direct constraint type classes (like `SglRegex`, `SglJson`) exported—constraints are passed as strings/parameters directly.

---

### 11. **Real-World Example**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775493190533-l4qytd/examples/frontend_language/usage/json_decode.py`

```python
@sgl.function
def character_gen(s, name):
    s += (
        name
        + " is a character in Harry Potter. Please fill in the following information about this character.\n"
    )
    s += sgl.gen("json_output", max_tokens=256, regex=character_regex)

# Usage:
state = character_gen.run(name="Hermione Granger")
```

Constraints can also be built from Pydantic models:

```python
from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import build_regex_from_object

class Wizard(BaseModel):
    name: str
    age: int
    weapon: Weapon

s += sgl.gen(
    "character",
    max_tokens=128,
    temperature=0,
    regex=build_regex_from_object(Wizard),
)
```

---

### Summary

**Constraint Flow:**
1. User calls `sgl.gen(..., regex="...", json_schema="...", dtype=type)`
2. Creates `SglGen` expression with `SglSamplingParams`
3. Interpreter resolves params via `_resolve_sampling_params()`
4. Backend (e.g., `RuntimeEndpoint`) serializes via `to_srt_kwargs()`
5. HTTP request sends `{"sampling_params": {"regex": "...", "json_schema": "..."}}`
6. Server-side `GrammarManager` receives constraints in `SamplingParams`
7. Dispatches to grammar backend (`outlines`, `xgrammar`, `llguidance`)
8. Grammar backend compiles constraint to finite state machine (FSM)
9. During decoding, FSM restricts token generation vocabulary at each step

490	    async def generate_request(
491	        self,
492	        obj: Union[GenerateReqInput, EmbeddingReqInput],
493	        request: Optional[fastapi.Request] = None,
494	    ):
495	        created_time = obj.received_time if obj.received_time else time.time()
496	        self.auto_create_handle_loop()
497	
498	        # Normalize the request
499	        obj.normalize_batch_and_arguments()
500	        if self.enable_trace:
501	            self._trace_request_start(obj, created_time, request)
502	        if self.server_args.language_only:
503	            self._handle_epd_disaggregation_encode_request(obj)
504	        if self.server_args.tokenizer_worker_num > 1:
505	            self._attach_multi_http_worker_info(obj)
506	
507	        # Log the request
508	        self.request_logger.log_received_request(obj, self.tokenizer, request)
509	
510	        async with self.is_pause_cond:
511	            await self.is_pause_cond.wait_for(lambda: not self.is_pause)
512	
513	        async with self.model_update_lock.reader_lock:
514	            await self._validate_and_resolve_lora(obj)
515	
516	            # Tokenize the request and send it to the scheduler
517	            if obj.is_single:
518	                tokenized_obj = await self._tokenize_one_request(obj)
519	                state = self._send_one_request(obj, tokenized_obj, created_time)
520	                async for response in self._wait_one_response(obj, state, request):
521	                    yield response
522	            else:
523	                async for response in self._handle_batch_request(
524	                    obj, request, created_time
525	                ):
526	                    yield response

528	    def _detect_input_format(
529	        self, texts: Union[str, List[str]], is_cross_encoder: bool
530	    ) -> InputFormat:
531	        """Detect the format of input texts for proper tokenization handling.
532	
533	        Returns:
534	            - InputFormat.SINGLE_STRING: Regular single text like "Hello world"
535	            - InputFormat.BATCH_STRINGS: Regular batch like ["Hello", "World"]
536	            - InputFormat.CROSS_ENCODER_PAIRS: Cross-encoder pairs like [["query", "document"]]
537	        """
538	        if isinstance(texts, str):
539	            return InputFormat.SINGLE_STRING
540	
541	        if (
542	            is_cross_encoder
543	            and len(texts) > 0
544	            and isinstance(texts[0], list)
545	            and len(texts[0]) == 2
546	        ):
547	            return InputFormat.CROSS_ENCODER_PAIRS
548	
549	        return InputFormat.BATCH_STRINGS
550	
551	    def _prepare_tokenizer_input(
552	        self, texts: Union[str, List[str]], input_format: InputFormat
553	    ) -> Union[List[str], List[List[str]]]:
554	        """Prepare input for the tokenizer based on detected format."""
555	        if input_format == InputFormat.SINGLE_STRING:
556	            return [texts]  # Wrap single string for batch processing
557	        elif input_format == InputFormat.CROSS_ENCODER_PAIRS:
558	            return texts  # Already in correct format: [["query", "doc"]]
559	        else:  # BATCH_STRINGS
560	            return texts  # Already in correct format: ["text1", "text2"]

562	    def _extract_tokenizer_results(
563	        self,
564	        input_ids: List[List[int]],
565	        token_type_ids: Optional[List[List[int]]],
566	        input_format: InputFormat,
567	        original_batch_size: int,
568	    ) -> Union[

570	        Tuple[List[List[int]], Optional[List[List[int]]]],
571	    ]:
572	        """Extract results from tokenizer output based on input format."""
573	
574	        # For single inputs (string or single cross-encoder pair), extract first element
575	        if (
576	            input_format in [InputFormat.SINGLE_STRING, InputFormat.CROSS_ENCODER_PAIRS]

580	            single_token_type_ids = token_type_ids[0] if token_type_ids else None

582	
583	        # For true batches, return as-is

1937	    def _request_has_grammar(self, obj: GenerateReqInput) -> bool:
1938	        return (

1940	            or obj.sampling_params.get("regex", None)

1945	    def collect_metrics(self, state: ReqState, recv_obj: BatchStrOutput, i: int):
1946	        completion_tokens = (
1947	            recv_obj.completion_tokens[i]
1948	            if getattr(recv_obj, "completion_tokens", None)
1949	            else 0
1950	        )
1951	
1952	        custom_labels = getattr(state.obj, "custom_labels", None)
1953	        labels = (
1954	            {**self.metrics_collector.labels, **custom_labels}
1955	            if custom_labels
1956	            else self.metrics_collector.labels
1957	        )
1958	        if (
1959	            state.first_token_time == 0.0
1960	            and self.disaggregation_mode != DisaggregationMode.PREFILL
1961	        ):
1962	            state.first_token_time = state.last_time = time.time()
1963	            state.first_token_time_perf = time.perf_counter()
1964	            state.last_completion_tokens = completion_tokens
1965	            self.metrics_collector.observe_time_to_first_token(
1966	                labels, state.first_token_time - state.created_time
1967	            )
1968	        else:
1969	            num_new_tokens = completion_tokens - state.last_completion_tokens
1970	            if num_new_tokens:
1971	                new_time = time.time()
1972	                interval = new_time - state.last_time
1973	                self.metrics_collector.observe_inter_token_latency(
1974	                    labels,
1975	                    interval,
1976	                    num_new_tokens,
1977	                )
1978	                state.last_time = new_time
1979	                state.last_completion_tokens = completion_tokens
1980	
1981	        if state.finished:
1982	            retraction_count = (
1983	                recv_obj.retraction_counts[i]
1984	                if getattr(recv_obj, "retraction_counts", None)
1985	                and i < len(recv_obj.retraction_counts)
1986	                else 0
1987	            )
1988	
1989	            # Get detailed cache breakdown if available
1990	            cached_tokens_details = None
1991	            if (
1992	                hasattr(recv_obj, "cached_tokens_details")
1993	                and recv_obj.cached_tokens_details
1994	            ):
1995	                cached_tokens_details = recv_obj.cached_tokens_details[i]
1996	
1997	            self.metrics_collector.observe_one_finished_request(
1998	                labels,
1999	                recv_obj.prompt_tokens[i],
2000	                completion_tokens,
2001	                recv_obj.cached_tokens[i],
2002	                state.finished_time - state.created_time,
2003	                self._request_has_grammar(state.obj),
2004	                retraction_count,
2005	                cached_tokens_details,
2006	            )
2007	
2008	    def dump_requests(self, state: ReqState, out_dict: dict):
2009	        self.dump_request_list.append(
2010	            (state.obj, out_dict, state.created_time, time.time())
2011	        )
2012

2017	            )
2018	            self._dump_data_to_file(
2019	                data_list=self.dump_request_list,
2020	                filename=filename,
2021	                log_message=f"Dump {len(self.dump_request_list)} requests to {filename}",
2022	            )
2023	            self.dump_request_list = []

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state

114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
